text: Replacing 32% of endurance training volume with explosive-strength training improves 5-km race time in well-trained endurance athletes over 9 weeks.
Explosive-strength training improves 5K performance through improved running economy and neuromuscular characteristics, not through changes in VO2max.

Simultaneous explosive-strength and endurance training improves running economy, maximal anaerobic running velocity (VMART), 20-m sprint speed, and 5-jump performance compared to endurance-only training.
Changes in 5K velocity correlate with changes in running economy (r = -0.54) and maximal anaerobic running velocity (r = 0.55).
Endurance-only training without explosive-strength work can lead to decreases in sprint speed and muscular power even as VO2max improves.
Explosive-strength training can improve 5K performance without increasing total training volume, by substituting a portion of endurance training.
